findings.title: Missing push trigger on main branch for critical CI workflows
findings.summary: The packaged-entrypoint and product-e2e workflows only trigger on pull requests and workflow dispatches, meaning they will not automatically run when code is merged to the main branch.
evidence: packages/audit-code/.github/workflows/packaged-entrypoint.yml:3 - The 'on:' block specifies workflow_dispatch and pull_request but omits a push event trigger for the main branch, leaving main branch commits untested post-merge.
packages/audit-code/.github/workflows/product-e2e.yml:3 - Similarly, the 'on:' block is missing a push event trigger for the main branch.

findings.title: Concurrency group in publish workflow allows parallel deployments
findings.summary: The publish-package workflow uses github.ref in its concurrency group, meaning simultaneous releases for different tags will run in parallel rather than queueing.
evidence: packages/audit-code/.github/workflows/publish-package.yml:29 - 'group: publish-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}' scopes concurrency by tag. Concurrent publishes for different tags will not be serialized, risking race conditions during npm publish.

findings.title: Floating version pin for npm installation in publish workflow
findings.summary: The publish workflow installs npm using a floating version pin (^11.5.1), which could break the deployment pipeline if a regression is introduced in a minor or patch update.
evidence: packages/audit-code/.github/workflows/publish-package.yml:67 - 'npm install -g npm@^11.5.1' uses a caret range rather than an exact version or SHA pin.
